reflection:
Yesterday was the post-workout crash you flagged yourself, and honestly your read on it was spot on. Bread panel plus DNS in the morning, 1572 calories on the whole day, then a flatline from 1pm to nearly 7pm. Your body literally told you what was missing and you wrote it in the could_be_better field. The takeaway is not guilt, it is confirmation that your instinct about food timing was right. Listen to it.

Recovery dropped to 42 with HRV at 30.8, three days of softening from 63 on Friday. The obvious culprits are the under-fueling around exercise plus a 481-step day. And the eye itching you noticed, you called it stage one allergies yourself. Don't dismiss it, it lines up with the recovery curve.

The speech keeps coming up. Three days running now. Worry about being interesting enough, Carolina being there ten days, doing too much. You're going to overdo it on the speech because you always do. The move is not to avoid it, it is to put a bounded block on the calendar so it stops bleeding into the rest of the day. The Turkey conf starts 30 May, you have time.

One thing for today. If you train or do DNS, eat a real meal with protein within the hour. That is the signal your own diary just gave you.
opener: post-workout crash and eye itching are signaling the same thing
focus_for_today: if you train today, eat a real meal with protein within the hour
